propositions: The rebar-gerrit-alerts SNS email subscription must be confirmed (PendingConfirmation=false) so alarms actually deliver.
No terraform drift-detection CI exists, allowing committed-but-unapplied HCL to sit unnoticed (the dc3f root cause).
A new least-privilege plan-only IAM role rebar-terraform-plan, assumable via GitHub OIDC, is added to run read-only terraform plans.
A drift-detection GitHub workflow runs terraform plan with -detailed-exitcode on PRs, pushes to main, a daily schedule, and manual dispatch, failing on non-empty plans.
A live-but-unmanaged alarm rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high with an empty alarm_actions list must be codified in HCL, wired to the SNS topic, and imported into state.
Terraform writes must follow the HCL merge and post-merge apply should be targeted to avoid sweeping in unrelated pre-existing instance user_data drift.
